close_reason: Correct: SecretValue intentionally omits Debug/Display/Clone to prevent accidental leaks
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:53: SecretValue is a struct wrapping Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> with NO Debug, Display, or Clone derives. This is correct and should be defended.

A contributor 'cleaning up' the missing derives (clippy::missing_debug_implementations is a real lint that complains about this) will produce a security regression: Debug on a Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> formats the bytes, putting the secret into the formatter's intermediate String (non-Zeroizing) and from there into wherever the Debug output is sent (logs, panic messages, eprintln!).

Defense: keep the existing 'Does not implement Debug...' line in the doc comment. If you ever do want a Debug impl, write a manual one that prints '<<SecretValue redacted>>' — never the bytes.
title: DEFEND: SecretValue intentionally lacks Debug/Display/Clone
